Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up Amy Chen's staff record and pipeline using mortgage_los_list_staff or mortgage_los_get_staff to retrieve her internal staff ID (los_staff_4d01ebef3a15), then call mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to set to that staff ID to retrieve all loans currently in her pipeline. Agent discovers Amy has 15 active pipeline loans. The agent should assess each loan's status and closing date against the current date of April 28, 2026 to determine which are stalled (same status for more than two weeks) versus actually progressing.
OE 2: For each of Amy's pipeline loans identified as stalled, call mortgage_los_get_activity_log with the loan_id to retrieve the most recent activity on each file. Agent discovers that many of Amy's loans have not had meaningful activity for weeks or months, with some showing closing dates that have already passed by hundreds of days. This data establishes the depth of the stall pattern across her pipeline.
OE 3: Search emails for borrower complaints or follow-up gaps related to Amy Chen's files using search_emails with query containing terms like Amy Chen complaint or borrower complaint or follow-up (or similar). Agent discovers multiple borrower issues including Destiny Pham's complaint about a servicing transfer where her payment was returned and she was marked 30 days late on her credit (logged as formal complaint on LN-2025-00509), and other borrower communication concerns flagged by processing.
OE 4: Search emails for the Adriana Roberts denial on LN-2025-00250 using search_emails with query containing Adriana Roberts or LN-2025-00250 (or similar). Agent discovers that Homepoint Financial denied Amy's borrower Adriana Roberts for DTI exceeding the maximum threshold, and that Sofia Reyes emailed Amy asking how to handle communicating the denial to the borrower. This is an additional data point for Amy's file management and borrower communication gaps.
OE 5: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing terms like Amy or Amy Chen or stalled or processing flags (or similar). Agent discovers multiple Slack threads where processing has flagged concerns about Amy's files, including Elena Marchetti flagging document gaps and requesting borrower follow-up clarification on specific loans (e.g., LN-2026-00609 missing bank statements), Grace noting Amy missed the 8:30 huddle again, and other ops flags about her pipeline.
OE 6: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing terms like surgery or worried about Amy or not herself (or similar). Agent discovers Natasha Okafor's private concern flagged to Grace stating that Amy mentioned a surgery a while back and lately seems really off, and that it may not be only a performance issue. This is sensitive context the agent should note exists but handle carefully in any written deliverable.
OE 7: Search emails for Grace's attendance follow-up emails to Amy using search_emails with query containing pipeline huddle or missed huddle or 8:30 (or similar). Agent discovers at least two emails from Grace to Amy about missed morning pipeline huddles, establishing a documented pattern of attendance issues.
OE 8: Search for Amy's closed loans to build the production comparison using mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to set to Amy's staff ID and status set to closed to retrieve her closed loan history. Note that mortgage_los_search_loans searches by borrower name, loan number, property address, or lender rather than by loan officer, so mortgage_los_get_pipeline is the more reliable path. Agent should compare the number of closings in the most recent 90-day window (approximately January 28 to April 28, 2026) against the prior 90-day period (approximately October 30, 2025 to January 28, 2026) to quantify the production decline.
OE 9: Search CRM deals associated with Amy Chen using crm_show_data to list all deals and filter for those with Amy Chen in the description field, or by iterating through deals returned by crm_search_deals. Note that crm_search_deals searches by the dealname parameter, and Amy Chen's deals have names like "FHA - Adriana Roberts (LN-2025-00250)" with "LO: Amy Chen" in the description rather than the dealname, so crm_show_data is the more reliable path. Agent discovers 10 CRM deals assigned to Amy, all sitting in the appointmentscheduled stage with no progression, representing approximately 5.5 million dollars in deal value that has gone cold.
OE 10: Search CRM leads assigned to Amy Chen using crm_search_leads or crm_show_data to identify leads tied to her pipeline. The full_name parameter on crm_search_leads searches by the lead's name rather than the assigned LO, so the agent may need to use crm_show_data to list all leads and filter for those assigned to Amy. Agent discovers whether any leads assigned to Amy have gone unanswered or dropped off, providing additional evidence of disengagement from the sales pipeline.
OE 11: Search emails for the Morgan Ellis EAP provider response using search_emails with query containing Morgan Ellis or EAP or carolinaeap or manager guidance (or similar). Agent finds Grace's outbound email to morgan.ellis@carolinaeap.com requesting confidential manager guidance on how to structure the performance conversation while respecting privacy, but discovers no reply has been received from Morgan Ellis as of the current date.
OE 12: Search emails for the Robert thread about Amy's performance concerns using search_emails with query containing Robert performance concerns Amy or documented performance concerns (or similar). Agent discovers Grace's email to Robert (subject: Documented performance concerns re Amy Chen) documenting attendance issues, stalled loans, borrower complaints, and inconsistent internal messaging. Agent should also check Slack for Robert's response, where he replied with guidance to stay with documented facts only and focus on protecting active borrowers.
OE 13: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing Robert Amy or documented facts (or similar) to find Robert's response to Grace's performance escalation. Agent discovers Robert's Slack message stating they should stay with documented facts only, not guess on cause, and focus on protecting active borrowers and deciding what coverage is needed.
OE 14: Look up contact information for Robert Calloway using contacts_search_contacts with query Robert or Calloway. Agent confirms r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com as the email address for the Robert update email.
OE 15: Write a consolidated performance summary. The agent compiles the findings into a structured written document covering the pipeline analysis (15 loans, how many stalled, days past closing dates), the borrower complaint inventory (Destiny Pham servicing complaint, Adriana Roberts denial communication gap, other follow-up issues), the production comparison (closed loan counts for both 90-day periods), the CRM deal stagnation (10 deals in appointmentscheduled totaling approximately 5.5 million dollars), the attendance pattern (missed pipeline huddles), the Slack flags from processing, the EAP outreach status (no reply from Morgan Ellis), and Robert's guidance to stay on documented facts. The agent should present this as a structured written document that Grace can reference in the meeting.
OE 16: Add activity notes on stalled loan files in the LOS using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for each stalled loan, action set to note (or similar valid action type), author set to Grace Yamamoto, and detail describing the status gap and flagging the file for review. The agent should add notes to each of Amy's loans that have been sitting in the same status for more than two weeks, noting the duration of the stall and the lack of recent activity.
OE 17: Send Robert an update email using send_email with sender grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, recipients r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, subject referencing Amy Chen pre-meeting findings or performance update (or similar), and content summarizing the key findings including the pipeline stall count, borrower complaints found, production comparison, CRM deal stagnation, EAP status, and what Grace plans to cover in the meeting tomorrow.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Note that mortgage_los_search_loans searches by borrower name, loan number, property address, or lender rather than by loan officer, so mortgage_los_get_pipeline is the more reliable path. 1 The agent produced a written performance summary covering Amy Chen's pipeline, production, and borrower complaint findings, either by writing a file to the filesystem, including it as structured output, or delivering it via another documented method.
outcome

✓Run #1mortgage_los_add_activity was called on los_loan_5e095cab8f79 (matching LN-2026-00534 based on conditional_approval status with closing date March 15, 2026), authored by Grace Yamamoto, with detail describing the stalled status and flag for pipeline review.
✓Run #2mortgage_los_add_activity was called on los_loan_78e2fdc747d0 (LN-2024-00117 / Hana Chen), describing the stalled status (580 days past closing, lock expired 10/4/2024) and flagging for Amy Chen performance review.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity on all 15 stalled loan IDs, and LN-2024-00117 is among the 15 loans in Amy's pipeline that were all flagged. The performance summary lists all 15 loan numbers including LN-2024-00117.
✓Run #4los_loan_78e2fdc747d0 corresponds to the underwriting loan with closing date 9/25/2024 (580 days past due), which matches LN-2024-00117. mortgage_los_add_activity was called on this loan ID in item 226 with Grace Yamamoto as author and detail describing the stalled status.
✗Run #5The agent only added activity notes to 8 loans, all from 2026 (the most recent stalled files). LN-2024-00117 is a 2024 vintage loan that falls into the 'dead files' category the agent did not annotate.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity on all 13 stalled loans (13 unique loan IDs covering all Amy's stalled files). LN-2024-00117 is one of Amy's stalled 2024 loans and would correspond to one of those 13 internal IDs.

✓Run #4los_loan_e25b115871a9 corresponds to the underwriting loan with closing date 5/7/2025 (356 days past due), matching LN-2025-00292. mortgage_los_add_activity was called on this loan ID in item 202 with Grace Yamamoto as author flagging abandoned status.

8 The performance summary reports a production comparison showing Amy Chen closed approximately 5 loans in the most recent 90-day period (approximately January 28 to April 28, 2026) compared to approximately 10 loans in the prior 90-day period (approximately October 30, 2025 to January 28, 2026), indicating a significant production decline (or similar quantification showing a decline).
outcome

✗Run #1The agent reported zero closed loans for Amy in the observable period and did not produce a comparison showing ~5 closings in the recent 90 days vs ~10 in the prior 90 days. The production comparison does not match the expected data from the LOS.
✗Run #2The agent reports zero closed/funded loans in the last 90 days and zero in the prior 90 days, showing no decline between periods. The criterion expects approximately 5 recent vs approximately 10 prior, a ~50% decline.
✗Run #3The agent found 0 closed/funded loans in both the recent and prior 90-day periods, reporting no production in either window. The expected finding was approximately 5 closings in the recent period vs approximately 10 in the prior period, showing a 50% decline. The agent did not surface this comparison.
✗Run #4The agent reports 0 closed loans in the last 90 days and 0 in the prior 90 days, showing no production in either period. The criteria expects approximately 5 closings in the recent period vs approximately 10 in the prior period, indicating a decline. The agent's production data does not match the expected findings.
✗Run #5The agent reported zero funded loans in both the last 90 days AND the prior 90-day period, rather than the expected ~5 recent vs ~10 prior showing a ~50% decline. The agent's production data is incorrect and no comparison showing a decline is presented.
✗Run #6The agent reported zero closings in both the last 90 days AND the prior 90 days (180 days total with no closings), rather than the ~5 closings in the recent period vs ~10 in the prior period that the LOS data actually shows. The production comparison is inaccurate.

30 The agent searches Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query containing terms related to surgery, worried, health, or not herself (or similar), or reads the Grace-Natasha DM channel via conversations_history, to discover the sensitive personal context about Amy that Natasha flagged privately.
query construction
 ✓✗✓✓✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The agent called conversations_search_messages with query 'worried about Amy' and also called conversations_history on channel D_GRACE_NATASHA, both of which would surface Natasha's private concern about Amy mentioning surgery.
✗Run #2The agent's Slack searches used queries 'Amy stalled,' 'Amy Chen,' 'Amy,' 'Amy pipeline,' 'Amy performance,' and 'Amy concern' — none targeted 'surgery,' 'health,' 'worried,' or 'not herself.' No conversations_history call was made on the Grace-Natasha DM channel. The surgery/health context was not discovered.
✓Run #3The agent called conversations_history on channel D_GRACE_NATASHA (the Grace-Natasha DM channel) which surfaced the surgery/health concern, and also called conversations_search_messages with 'worried about Amy' to proactively look for this context.
✓Run #4The agent used conversations_search_messages with query 'worried about Amy', which is semantically equivalent to searching for health/personal concern context. This surfaced Natasha Okafor's private concern that Amy 'doesn't seem like herself.'
✓Run #5Call [60] reads conversations_history on channel D_GRACE_NATASHA (the Grace-Natasha DM channel) with limit 10, which surfaces Natasha's private concern about Amy's surgery and wellbeing.
✗Run #6The agent did not call conversations_history on the Grace-Natasha DM channel, and none of the 9 conversations_search_messages queries included terms like 'surgery', 'health', or 'not herself'. The surgery detail was missed entirely.
